depends_on.context: When a Figma read or export feeds a design decision, apply sk-design-interface's anti-default judgment before deciding
The optional Figma MCP (Framelink figma manual) is invoked through Code Mode when MCP context is needed

enhances.context: Figma exports, DESIGN.md, variables, and screenshots feed application implementation that sk-code governs and verifies

siblings.context: Sibling terminal-driven design tool with a daemon model, gated mutating verbs, and an sk-design-interface handoff
Sibling CLI-primary plus MCP-via-Code-Mode skill sharing the same router and gating pattern

key_topics: figma-ds-cli binary (and the unrelated figma-cli npm collision)
safe (plugin) vs yolo (app.asar patch) connect modes
optional Framelink figma MCP via Code Mode
derived.causal_summary: mcp-figma teaches a terminal agent to drive Figma Desktop through the silships figma-cli, published to npm as figma-ds-cli (the npm package literally named figma-cli is an unrelated tool by unic, bin 'figma' - never install it). figma-cli has no MCP of its own; it drives the live Figma Desktop session via a local daemon (HTTP 127.0.0.1:3456, X-Daemon-Token). Connect is either safe (FigCli plugin, no patch - the default) or yolo (patches Figma app.asar, CDP 9222 - gated, rollback via unpatch). The CLI is primary for authoring/modifying/exporting in Figma; an optional Figma MCP (the community Framelink figma manual already in this repo's Code Mode) is opt-in for pulling design data into the agent for codegen. Commands are gated read-only / mutating / destructive, with eval/raw/run treated as arbitrary mutation and local exports requiring explicit no-overwrite paths. Pairs with sk-design-interface (design judgment) and sk-code (adapting exports into an app). Modeled on mcp-chrome-devtools and mcp-open-design.
